target.name: Majestic Labs
target.founder: Ofer Shacham, Sha Rabii, Masumi Reynders

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Majestic Labs is a Series A chip design company founded by Ofer Shacham, Sha Rabii, and Masumi Reynders, operating in a competitive frontier-semiconductor landscape populated by 8 private peers and anchored by 3 major public comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

Two valuation methods apply to this analysis. Stage-progression anchors to the company's $100M+ total raised, applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A-to-later-stage step-ups, yielding $200M. Peer-median analysis draws from four same-sector, same-stage companies: Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), Substrate ($1.0B), and Rain AI ($500M). This cohort yields a median of $1.5B with a range of $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiple (median 20.6x across the three public tickers) cannot be applied due to absence of revenue disclosure for the target.

The two applicable methods produce a triangulated valuation range of $170M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $850M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around commercialization trajectory, IP defensibility, and competitive positioning relative to well-funded peers like Etched. Stage-progression suggests conservative floor pricing; peer medians reflect sector enthusiasm but significant valuation volatility. Without disclosed revenue or near-term commercialization milestones, the low-to-median band ($170M–$850M) likely better reflects pre-revenue risk.
